channel.title: Microsoft Agent Framework on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Microsoft Agent Framework on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: Announcing: GitHub Copilot and Claude Code connectors in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes generally available connectors for GitHub Copilot and Claude Code. These connectors let .NET and Python agents delegate coding tasks to either system without custom adapter code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Multi-agent orchestration patterns including Magentic in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available for multi-agent orchestration patterns, including the dynamic Magentic pattern. The announcement cites benchmark results for Magentic, including 38% on GAIA and competitive results on AssistantBench and WebArena.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Agent Harness in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now has general availability for Agent Harness, the production runtime for agents built with the framework. Preview harness users can move the same agent code into production with backward compatibility.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Tracing for Microsoft Agent Framework (Python and .NET)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now provides public preview tracing support for Microsoft Agent Framework in Python and .NET. The feature lets developers observe agent reasoning, tool calls, and model interactions during execution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: CodeAct pattern and Hyperlight containers in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes the CodeAct pattern in public preview. It lets agents execute multi-step plans as a single code block instead of sequential tool calls, which reduced end-to-end latency in representative workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Episodic procedural memory in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now has episodic procedural memory in public preview. It stores successful task patterns so agents can reuse past action sequences, tool choices, and related context on similar future requests.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Agent Channel for Microsoft Agent Framework multi-agent communication
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes Agent Channel in public preview for multi-agent communication. It provides a built-in way for agents to exchange typed messages using request/response or fire-and-forget patterns without custom routing code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: DevUI Agent Inspector for Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes DevUI Agent Inspector in public preview. It provides a local debugging view for agent runs with a tree of model calls, tool invocations, and intermediate state, plus controls to pause, replay, and edit prompts during execution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description:
Part 3 of the Microsoft Agent Framework series shows how to build .NET AI agents using tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows. It ties together the earlier parts into the framework’s main building blocks for agent development.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Building an AI-Powered Conference App with .NET’s Composable AI Stack
item.description:
The post describes building ConferencePulse, an AI-powered conference assistant, using .NET’s composable AI stack. It highlights Microsoft.Extensions.AI, DataIngestion, VectorData, Model Context Protocol, and Microsoft Agent Framework as the main components.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available at version 1.0 for .NET and Python. The release includes stable APIs, long-term support, multi-agent orchestration, multi-provider model support, and cross-runtime interoperability via A2A.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Memory in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now has a preview memory feature for managed long-term memory. It also integrates natively with Microsoft Agent Framework and LangGraph, without requiring external databases to provision, scale, or secure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description:
Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on April 20, 2027 and replaced by Microsoft Agent Framework. The notice says the replacement is a single orchestration platform with new capabilities, but the provided excerpt does not include migration details.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description:
Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available. It adds VS Code support for Microsoft Agent Framework, with agent creation from templates or GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry.

  • Source: Azure Updates
